ddls:
CREATE TABLE "academic_programs" (
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each academic program */,
  "school_college" TEXT /* Name of the school or college offering the program */,
  "total_courses" INTEGER /* Total number of required courses for the program */,
  "program_name" TEXT /* Name of the academic program */,
  "program_degree" TEXT /* Degree offered by the program (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's) */,
  "program_description" TEXT /* Description of the academic program */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "course_requirements" (
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the course requirement entry */,
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic program */,
  "course_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the course type */,
  "course_count" REAL /* Number of courses needed for this requirement (can be fractional) */,
  "min_grade" TEXT /* Minimum grade required for each course */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes about the requirement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_requirements_program_id FOREIGN KEY ("program_id") REFERENCES academic_programs ("program_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_requirements_course_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_type_id") REFERENCES course_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "course_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course type */,
  "course_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the course type (e.g., English, Lab Science) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the course type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the course type (e.g., core, elective) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_name" TEXT /* Name of the course */,
  "course_description" TEXT /* Description of the course */,
  "credits" REAL /* Number of credits for the course */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the course type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_courses_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES course_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "program_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the update record */,
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic program being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the update */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who made the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_program_updates_program_id FOREIGN KEY ("program_id") REFERENCES academic_programs ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Name of the student */,
  "program_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic program */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date of enrollment */,
  "expected_graduation_date" TEXT /* Expected date of graduation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_program_id FOREIGN KEY ("program_id") REFERENCES academic_programs ("program_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "student_courses" (
  "student_course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student course entry */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the student */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the course */,
  "grade" TEXT /* Grade earned by the student */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date of course completion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_courses_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_student_courses_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)
